name: Alto de Alcola — circular desde Cofrentes
region_intro: Hiking around Cofrentes offers diverse terrain, characterized by unique volcanic landscapes, dramatic riverine environments, and extensive forests. The region is situated at the confluence of the Cabriel and Júcar rivers, featuring spectacular canyons and the accessible Cerro de Agras, an extinct volcano. Trails navigate through varied elevations, from river valleys to higher viewpoints, providing a range of experiences for hikers. Best hiking trails around Cofrentes The most popular hiking route is The Castle of Chirel (PR-CV 382), a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete, offering views of the historic castle and surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Campichuelo Trail, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. This route provides scenic views of Cofrentes Castle and the Cabriel River. Local hikers also love the La Noria, Casas de Alcance, Cofrentes y el Río Cabriel — circular desde Hervideros de Cofrentes, an 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail leading through volcanic landscapes and river canyons, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Hiking around Cofrentes is defined by volcanic features, deep river canyons, and diverse forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in Cofrentes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Cofrentes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What makes the hiking trails around Cofrentes unique?
A: Cofrentes offers a distinctive hiking experience due to its unique volcanic landscapes, particularly the accessible Cerro de Agras, an extinct volcano. The region is also defined by the dramatic confluence of the Cabriel and Júcar rivers, featuring spectacular canyons and diverse forests, providing a wide range of natural scenery.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Cofrentes, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails in Cofrentes. The routes cater to various skill levels, with 7 easy trails perfect for a relaxed stroll, 11 moderate routes for those seeking a bit more challenge, and 3 difficult trails for experienced hikers looking for a strenuous adventure.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Cofrentes?
A: Yes, Cofrentes features several excellent circular routes. One notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1130614671/la-noria-casas-de-alcance-cofrentes-y-el-rio-cabriel-circular-desde-hervideros-de-cofrentes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Noria, Casas de Alcance, Cofrentes y el Río Cabriel — circular desde Hervideros de Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.6-mile (13.8 km) trail that guides you through volcanic landscapes and river canyons, starting from the Hervideros area.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hikers can discover a variety of natural landmarks and stunning viewpoints. You'll find impressive gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5367776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Buitre&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2850716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cintos de la Cortada&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking views of the Júcar River. The area also boasts waterfalls such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3216823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo de Cortes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles accessible via hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. The Cofrentes Castle, perched atop an extinct volcano, offers a unique historical backdrop. You can explore the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2751423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chirel Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which is a prominent landmark. The trail &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e817941873/the-castle-of-chirel-pr-cv-382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Castle of Chirel (PR-CV 382)&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.1-mile (9.8 km) route that provides excellent views of this historic site and the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Cofrentes?
A: Yes, Cofrentes offers several easy trails suitable for families. While specific family-designated routes aren't listed, the 7 easy trails available provide gentle terrain and shorter distances, perfect for outings with children. These routes often explore the less strenuous parts of the river valleys and forests.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Cofrentes?
A: Many trails in Cofrentes are suitable for dogs, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check local regulations for specific trail restrictions before you go.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cofrentes?
A: The hiking routes in Cofrentes are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning river views, and the unique volcanic landscapes that make each trail a memorable experience.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Cofrentes Castle?
A: Yes, several trails provide excellent vantage points for viewing the historic Cofrentes Castle. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e815793844/el-campichuelo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Campichuelo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.6-mile (10.7 km) path, offers scenic views of the castle and the Cabriel River as you hike.

Q: Are there any trails that start near the Hervideros thermal springs?
A: Yes, the area around the Hervideros thermal springs is a popular starting point for hikes. The circular route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1130614671/la-noria-casas-de-alcance-cofrentes-y-el-rio-cabriel-circular-desde-hervideros-de-cofrentes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Noria, Casas de Alcance, Cofrentes y el Río Cabriel&lt;/a&gt; begins from Hervideros, allowing you to explore the volcanic and riverine landscapes directly from this well-known location.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cofrentes?
A: The best time to hike in Cofrentes is typically during the spring and autumn months. The weather is milder, making it ideal for exploring the trails. Summers can be very hot, especially in the river canyons, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler temperatures, particularly at higher elevations.
tips: It is a castle of Islamic origin dating from the 11th century built on one of the chimneys of the Cerro Agrás volcano and on the banks of the Cabriel river. It can be visited daily by appointment at the tourist office.
It is the starting point for many hiking trails and a good place to observe fauna linked to the river.
There are some nice views of the Embarcadero at this point. If you have time, you can take a boat tour on the river that takes just over an hour.
The top is 830 meters high. The climb is quite steep but the panoramic views are amazing. I invite you to take a seat and enjoy this magnificent place.
